hints.Mutato: 'Mutato' is a perfect passive participle in the ablative — part of an ablative absolute
hints.nomine: 'nomine' is a noun in the ablative meaning 'name'
hints.fabula: 'fabula' is a noun — the subject of the sentence

hints.to know: 'to know' is 'narrare' is not 'to know' — 'to know' is 'scire' or 'noscere'

steps.prompt: What tense, mood, and voice is 'narratur'?

hints.Present active indicative: Present active would be 'narrat' — 'narratur' with -tur ending is passive
hints.Perfect passive indicative: Perfect passive would be 'narrata est' — 'narratur' is present passive

hints.nascor, nasci, natus sum: These are the principal parts of 'nasci' (to be born)
hints.nato, natare, natavi, natatum: These are the principal parts of 'natare' (to swim)
hints.nosco, noscere, novi, notum: These are the principal parts of 'noscere' (to know)

hints.Ablative: Ablative of 'fabula' would also be 'fabula', but here it is the subject of the passive verb
hints.Vocative: Vocative would be 'fabula', but context shows it is the subject — nominative

hints.2nd: 2nd declension nouns end in -us/-um — 'fabula' with -a ending is 1st declension
hints.3rd: 3rd declension has varied endings — 'fabula' is a typical 1st declension -a noun
hints.5th: 5th declension nouns end in -es — 'fabula' is 1st declension

hints.Nominative: Nominative of 'nomen' is 'nomen' — 'nomine' with -e ending is ablative

hints.1st: 1st declension nouns end in -a — 'nomen' with genitive 'nominis' is 3rd declension
hints.2nd: 2nd declension neuter nouns end in -um — 'nomen' is 3rd declension neuter
hints.5th: 5th declension nouns end in -es — 'nomen' is 3rd declension

reveal.gloss: changed / with (the name) having been changed

hints.Ablative of means: Ablative of means uses a bare noun — 'Mutato nomine' is participle + noun forming an ablative absolute
hints.Ablative of manner: Ablative of manner often uses 'cum' — 'Mutato nomine' with a participle is an ablative absolute
hints.Ablative of agent: Ablative of agent uses 'a/ab' + ablative — this is an ablative absolute

translation: With the name changed, the story is told about you
